contentHtml:
Getting arrested and charged with a DUI in Burbank is often an intimidating and upsetting experience for most people.

After a DUI arrest, people often don’t understand the actual charges or what they can do to defend themselves.

Working with a skilled DUI defense lawyer in Burbank will protect your rights during this stressful time.

Contact our team of diligent lawyers today at Farris Law Firm at (818) 861-0861, and let us help you defend against your DUI charge.

Why Should I Hire Farris Law Firm for My DUI Case?

At Farris Law Firm, we have experience defending our Burbank community as they attempt to navigate the criminal justice system after prosecutors charge them with a DUI in California.

Founded in 2024 by husband and wife Charles and Beatrice Farris, Farris Law Firm uses its team’s experience managing thousands of misdemeanor and criminal cases to deliver positive results to its new clients.

Our firm prioritizes our local community in the Valley. We give each client the focus and dedication to their case that we believe they rightfully deserve.

We start each client relationship with a free consultation, during which we learn more about the client’s charges and circumstances before providing our guidance on the best path forward for their case.

Our office is conveniently located in downtown Burbank on North Third Street, just down the street from the Burbank Courthouse.

Call our team today if you or your loved one faces DUI charges in Burbank. Let us help you build a strong defense for your case.

What Is a DUI in California?

The California Vehicle Code makes driving a vehicle under the influence of drugs or alcohol illegal.

The classification of a drug in California includes any medication that impairs a person’s ability to drive, including prescription and over-the-counter medications.

California legislation identifies the following blood alcohol concentration (BAC)  level specifications for alcohol when classifying a DUI:

  • BAC of 0.08 Percent or Higher – If a person is 21 years of age or older and is operating a personal vehicle, a BAC level of 0.08 or higher is considered a DUI.
  • BAC of 0.04 Percent or Higher – The BAC level drops to 0.04 percent or higher for a DUI if the driver is driving a vehicle that requires a commercial driver’s license or if the person is driving a passenger for hire.
  • BAC of 0.01 Percent or Higher – A person under the age of 21 or already on probation for a DUI can be charged with a DUI if they have a BAC of 0.01 percent or higher.

FAQs About DUIs in Burbank

What Are the DUI Laws in California?

Drivers need to know the local laws in California and how they relate to DUI charges.

Admin Per Se Law

California has an Admin Per Se (APS) law, meaning law enforcement does not have to prove the driver showed any signs of impairment if they test for an illegal amount of alcohol or drugs in their system.

The APS law states that law enforcement can suspend or revoke a person’s driver’s license if they refuse or fail to take a blood or breath chemical test to derive their BAC level or if their blood or breath chemical test shows a BAC level at or above the legal limits.

Implied Consent

California is an implied consent state. This means that drivers in California consent to permitting law enforcement to test them for the use of alcohol or drugs if law enforcement pulls a person over under the suspicion of driving under the influence.

A driver can refuse to take a blood or breath chemical test when pulled over; however, refusing a test could result in being penalized with fines, immediate imprisonment, or having their driver’s license suspended or revoked.

What Do I Do if I Get Arrested for a DUI in Burbank?

Take the proper actions to ensure you protect your rights after a DUI arrest.

Do Not Accept the Initial Offer

A prosecution team may attempt to intimidate defendants into believing they should accept the first offer the prosecution gives them.

Do not accept the initial offer, or any offers, from the prosecution without seeking professional legal guidance on your defense. Committing to an offer or a plea deal without trusted legal consultation could weaken your defense and put you at a legal disadvantage.

Contact a Lawyer

Contact a lawyer as soon as possible after facing DUI charges in Burbank.

Having a skilled and knowledgeable DUI defense lawyer on your side will protect your rights during this stressful time while working to reduce or eliminate any potential penalties or restrictions you may face for your DUI charge.

Work With a Trusted DUI Defense Lawyer in Burbank

Most people feel scared and upset after a DUI arrest in Burbank.

They often do not understand what the charges against them mean or what they can do to better defend themselves. If they meet with the prosecution alone without seeking their own legal counsel, the prosecution’s tactics could intimidate them and they may think they need to take the initial plea or offer.

At Farris Law Firm, we understand how challenging it is for people and their loved ones to try and navigate the worrying aftermath of a DUI charge alone. We strive to ease this stressful time by evaluating your case’s facts and helping you build a strong defense against your charges.

If you or a loved one has been charged with a DUI in Burbank, contact our team at Farris Law Firm today at (818) 861-0861. Let us help you build a strong defense for your case.
